body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:interrupt

(Re-derived 2026-05-26 against opencode CLI v1.14.50 and the CURRENT drive-opencode-interactive.sh, which has since gained a live-TUI tmux path â€” `run_live()`. The three axes are unchanged from the prior verdict, but the gap is now NARROWER than previously described: the live-TUI scaffolding exists; only a mid-turn keystroke step type is missing.)

## Scenario

user-esc-interrupt: the user cancels a long in-flight turn with the interrupt key before the agent finishes; state resolves cleanly to `ready`, and the next prompt proves the parser reset. User-observable signal (scenario-meanings): `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`; state does not stick in `working` post-interrupt, then a clean recovery arc. Primitive exercised: Interrupt-during-turn â€” the agent CLI exposes a cancel signal and resets cleanly so the next prompt drives a fresh `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` arc.

## Agent supports it (yes)

opencode's interactive TUI binds Escape / Ctrl-C to cancel the in-flight turn; an aborted turn reports a model finish reason of `interrupted`, persisted as a `step-finish` part in the store. The agent CAN be driven into the readyâ†’workingâ†’[interrupt]â†’ready arc the scenario exercises. (CLI v1.14.50 confirmed installed; no change to the cancel surface.)

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter â€” the daemon reads session/turn state straight from the SQLite store, NOT via the claudecode `IsUserInterrupt` / `LastWasUserInterrupt` / `recordUserInterruptFlags` text-marker machinery (that path lives only under core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/ and does not apply here). For opencode the interrupt is observed structurally: parser.go:158 `case "interrupted"` maps a `step-finish` part with reason=interrupted â†’ `turn_done` (comment at parser.go:90: reason=interrupted â†’ user cancelled (Ctrl+C) â†’ emit turn_done), and watcher.go:435 `isTerminalPart` accepts `interrupted` among its terminal reasons {stop, interrupted, length, error, content-filter}. Both are exercised by tests (parser_test.go:347 TestParser_StepFinish_Interrupted_TurnDone; watcher_test.go:582 interruptedâ†’true). So once a real interrupted-turn step-finish part lands in the store the daemon settles the session workingâ†’ready cleanly. The daemon is NOT the blocker â€” genuine `daemon_capability: full`, distinct from the sibling `turn-aborted-by-error` cell (incapable: an error abort lands on message.data.error and never persists a step-finish part).

## Why this cell is STILL not recordable today (driver_capability: gap:interrupt)

The scenario requires interrupting an IN-FLIGHT turn with a live keystroke. As of 2026-05-26 the driver has TWO paths:

1. **Headless path** (default, lines 296-449): each `send` shells out to a BLOCKING `opencode run` subprocess that returns only after the turn is over â€” structurally there is no in-flight turn to interrupt by the time control returns. Step grammar: `send` / `wait_turn` (no-op) / `sleep`. No way to express a mid-turn ESC.

2. **Live-TUI path** `run_live()` (lines 99-294): launches the real opencode TUI in a tmux pane, polls readiness on the "Ask anything" prompt, sends prompts via `tmux send-keys`, detects turn completion by counting `step-finish` parts in the store, and exports the session(s). THIS is the recordable surface for a mid-turn interrupt â€” a turn streams live and a keystroke could reach it. BUT its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch (lines 195-223) handles only `send` / `wait_turn` / `reset_session` / `slash` / `sleep`. **There is NO `interrupt` step and NO `keys` step** â€” no helper sends a raw Escape (or Ctrl-C) into the pane. `oc_send` always appends Enter; nothing delivers a bare control key. Moreover the router at line 355 only enters `run_live` when a `reset_session` or `slash` step is present, so an interrupt-shaped recipe would not even reach the TUI path.

So the recently-added live-TUI scaffolding (tmux launch, readiness poll, store-polling turn detection, multi-session export) removes the structural blocker the prior assessment described, but the actual interrupt PRIMITIVE is still absent. The gap is real and named precisely below.

## The exact missing primitive

A `keys`/`interrupt` step in `run_live()` that fires a raw key event mid-stream â€” the opencode analogue of the claudecode driver's `step_interrupt` (`tmux send-keys -t "$CURRENT_TMUX" Escape`, drive-claudecode-interactive.sh:334) and generic `step_keys` (`tmux send-keys -t "$CURRENT_TMUX" $keys`, line 354). Concretely the unblock is small now that `run_live` exists:

1. Add an `oc_keys`/`oc_interrupt` helper: `tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Escape` (no Enter, unlike `oc_send`).
2. Add `interrupt` (and ideally generic `keys`) to the `run_live` `case "$TYPE"` dispatch.
3. Extend the router on line 355 so an `interrupt`/`keys` step also routes to `run_live` (currently only `reset_session`/`slash` do).

The recipe would then: `send` a long prompt â†’ `sleep` a beat so the turn is mid-stream â†’ `interrupt` (Escape) â†’ `wait_turn` for the interrupted step-finish â†’ `send` a short follow-up to prove the recovery arc. The daemon already parses reason=interrupted â†’ turn_done, so the workingâ†’ready settle and the recovery arc in expected.jsonl would validate once the keystroke can be delivered.

## Not caught by recipe-lint

The recipe is left `applicable:false` with no `script`, so there is no step-TYPE for recipe-lint to reject â€” the gap is the ABSENCE of a live-keystroke interrupt capability in the driver, the same shape as the opencode model-switch-midsession gap:model-select, which recipe-lint likewise cannot catch.

## Matrix

The coverage matrix already records driver_capability: gap:interrupt (notes: "parser handles step-finish reason=interrupted, but the headless send-only driver can't fire a mid-turn ESC; needs a live-REPL opencode driver (tracked: #492)"). This re-derivation RATIFIES that verdict (no drift on the three axes). One refinement worth noting to the maintainer: the note's premise ("needs a live-REPL opencode driver") is now partly satisfied â€” the live-REPL/TUI driver EXISTS (`run_live`); what remains is adding the `interrupt`/`keys` step type and routing to it. The unblock is an extend-driver task, not a from-scratch driver build.

## Confidence

0.85: the live-TUI driver path and its exact step grammar (`send|wait_turn|reset_session|slash|sleep`, no interrupt/keys, router gated on reset_session/slash) are read directly from the current driver source; the parser/watcher reason=interrupted â†’ turn_done handling is read directly from parser.go / watcher.go and is test-covered. Not higher only because the unblocked recording itself (a real interrupted step-finish part flowing through to a workingâ†’ready settle in a TUI session) has not yet been captured â€” that is the extend-driver task this gap defers.
caveats: LIVE EVIDENCE 2026-05-28 (driver step now ported, 23ab346e; oc_interrupt sends a bare Escape and oc_turn_total counts reason=interrupted): the interrupt step type EXISTS and routes to run_live, so driver_capability is `ready` as CODE. BUT a third+fourth live attempt against lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored @32768 ctx STILL produced ZERO step-finish reason=interrupted. Root cause confirmed in opencode.db: the local model stalls ~122-125s PRE-STREAM before emitting any step-start (attempt 3 delegated to the `task` subagent tool â†’ step-finish reason=tool-calls + a MessageAbortedError on the message; attempt 4 with tools forbidden in the prompt streamed a 14547-char essay but only AFTER a 125s pre-stream stall, then ended NATURALLY on step-finish reason=stop). In BOTH the long-essay/sleep-20s/ESC recipe runs the Escape landed in the pre-stream dead zone (no in-flight stream to cancel), so it was a no-op and the turn completed on `stop`/`tool-calls` â€” never `interrupted`. The driver's oc_interrupt false-settles because it cannot distinguish a natural terminal step-finish from an interrupted one. NET: not recordable end-to-end on this model â€” the elicitation blocker is the model/provider's ~2min pre-stream latency + non-interrupted termination, NOT the daemon (parser handles reason=interrupted; test-covered) and NOT the driver code (step ported). Recipe degraded to applicable:false with a scope_note. UNBLOCK: a provider/model whose TUI mid-stream Escape genuinely persists a step-finish reason=interrupted (and that begins streaming promptly so a bounded ESC lands mid-turn), and hardening oc_interrupt to assert an actual `interrupted` part rather than any terminal step-finish so a no-op ESC fails loudly.

Gap is narrower than the prior assessment described: drive-opencode-interactive.sh now has a live-TUI tmux path (run_live, lines 99-294) â€” the structural 'blocking opencode run, no in-flight turn to cancel' blocker is gone. What remains missing is a single step type, not a whole driver.

Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode supports mid-turn cancel and the daemon parses step-finish reason=interrupted â†’ turn_done (parser.go:158, watcher.go:435; test-covered). opencode does NOT use claudecode's IsUserInterrupt/LastWasUserInterrupt text-marker path â€” it observes the interrupt structurally from the SQLite step-finish part.

recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap: the recipe is applicable:false with no script, so there is no step-TYPE to reject; the missing capability is a live-keystroke interrupt step in run_live (same shape as the opencode model-switch-midsession gap:model-select).

sources.note: run_live() (lines 99-294) is a real live-TUI tmux driver (launch opencode, poll 'Ask anything' readiness, send-keys prompts, count step-finish parts for turn detection, export sessions), but its case "$TYPE" dispatch (lines 195-223) handles only send|wait_turn|reset_session|slash|sleep â€” NO interrupt and NO keys step; oc_send always appends Enter, nothing delivers a bare Escape/Ctrl-C. Router (line 355) only enters run_live for reset_session/slash, so an interrupt recipe would not even reach the TUI path.

step_interrupt (line 334: tmux send-keys ... Escape) and generic step_keys (line 354: tmux send-keys ... $keys, no implicit Enter) are the reference primitives the opencode run_live path lacks â€” the exact missing step type to port (gap:interrupt).
line 158 `case "interrupted"` maps step-finish reason=interrupted â†’ turn_done (comment line 90: reason=interrupted â†’ user cancelled (Ctrl+C) â†’ emit turn_done) â€” daemon_capability:full.
isTerminalPart (line 435) accepts reason in {stop, interrupted, length, error, content-filter} â€” an interrupted step-finish part is a terminal turn signal that settles workingâ†’ready.

TestParser_StepFinish_Interrupted_TurnDone (line 347) asserts reason=interrupted â†’ turn_done with a token contribution; watcher_test.go:582 asserts the interrupted step-finish is a terminal part â€” daemon observability is test-anchored, not doc-inferred.

coverage cell already records driver_capability:gap:interrupt (tracked #492); this re-derivation ratifies the three axes. Maintainer note: the 'needs a live-REPL opencode driver' premise is now partly satisfied (run_live exists) â€” remaining work is adding the interrupt/keys step, an extend-driver task.
